name: Montesclaros Sanctuary and Alto del Bardal Loop
name_original: Fontibre, Villacantid, Alto de Bardal, Monasterio de Montes Claros, Reinosa – ruta circular
description: Cycle a moderate 42.9-mile route through Cantabria, visiting Fontibre, Alto del Bardal, and Monasterio de Montes Claros with diverse scenery
region_intro: Road cycling around Hermandad De Campoo De Suso offers diverse terrain within the scenic Cantabrian Mountains. The region is characterized by demanding mountain climbs, picturesque valleys, and well-maintained paved roads. Cyclists can explore routes around the Ebro Reservoir and the Fontibre spring, providing varied experiences from challenging ascents to more moderate loops. This landscape provides a compelling blend of rugged mountains and serene natural features. Best road cycling routes around Hermandad De Campoo De Suso The most popular road cycling route is Alto Campoo - Fuente del Chivo Climb, a 37.5 miles (60.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 21 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain towards the Alto Campoo ski resort. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Montesclaros Sanctuary and Alto del Bardal Loop, a moderate 42.9 miles (69.0 km) path. This route offers a balanced challenge with rewarding views of the Cantabrian landscape. Local road cyclists also love the Nestares, Alto del Bardal, Mataporquera, Matamorosa – circular route, a 39.3 miles (63.2 km) trail leading through valleys and past traditional villages, often completed in about 3 hours 3 minutes. Road cycling around Hermandad De Campoo De Suso is defined by the Cantabrian Mountains, the Ebro Reservoir, and demanding climbs like those leading to Alto Campoo. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult mountain ascents. The routes in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 1600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Hermandad De Campoo De Suso's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso?
A: There are over 60 road cycling routes in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso, offering a wide range of experiences from moderate loops to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso?
A: The region is known for its diverse topography within the scenic Cantabrian Mountains. You'll find demanding mountain climbs, picturesque valleys, and well-maintained paved roads. Routes often feature significant elevation gains, especially those leading to areas like Alto Campoo.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Hermandad De Campoo De Suso is highly regarded for its demanding climbs. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005123044/alto-campoo-fuente-del-chivo-climb" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Campoo - Fuente del Chivo Climb&lt;/a&gt;, covering over 60 km with substantial elevation, are perfect for experienced cyclists. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40344034" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collada de Carmona – Palombera Pass loop from Castillo de San Vicente&lt;/a&gt;, which features nearly 1900 meters of ascent.

Q: Can I find moderate road cycling routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. Many routes offer a balanced challenge with rewarding views. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004549582/montesclaros-sanctuary-and-alto-del-bardal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montesclaros Sanctuary and Alto del Bardal Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 69 km path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005124609/nestares-alto-del-bardal-mataporquera-matamorosa-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nestares, Alto del Bardal, Mataporquera, Matamorosa – circular route&lt;/a&gt; is another popular moderate option.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005124609/nestares-alto-del-bardal-mataporquera-matamorosa-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nestares, Alto del Bardal, Mataporquera, Matamorosa – circular route&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, offering a 63 km loop through valleys and traditional villages. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004549582/montesclaros-sanctuary-and-alto-del-bardal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montesclaros Sanctuary and Alto del Bardal Loop&lt;/a&gt; is also a popular circular option.

Q: What scenic viewpoints can I discover while road cycling?
A: The region offers breathtaking views. You can cycle past highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/586766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedrasluengas Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4566932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Moral Plateau&lt;/a&gt;. The Mirador de la Joyanca also provides impressive vistas of the Campoo valley. Many routes traverse the Cantabrian Mountains, ensuring panoramic scenery.

Q: Are there any natural landmarks to see along the routes?
A: Yes, the area is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2573626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Ebro River&lt;/a&gt; at Fontibre, and the Ebro Reservoir offers picturesque cycling loops. The majestic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tres Mares Peak&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Labra&lt;/a&gt; are also prominent natural features in the region.

Q: What is the best season for road biking in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso?
A: While specific seasonal recommendations aren't provided, the region's mountainous terrain suggests that spring, summer, and early autumn would generally offer the most favorable weather conditions for road cycling, avoiding winter snows at higher elevations like Alto Campoo.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Hermandad De Campoo De Suso?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 37 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from challenging mountain climbs to scenic valley routes, and the well-maintained paved roads that make for an excellent road cycling experience.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through traditional villages?
A: Yes, some routes will take you through charming traditional mountain villages. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005124609/nestares-alto-del-bardal-mataporquera-matamorosa-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nestares, Alto del Bardal, Mataporquera, Matamorosa – circular route&lt;/a&gt; leads through several such villages, allowing you to experience the local culture and historical charm.

Q: What is the typical duration for road cycling routes in this region?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and elevation gain. For example, a difficult route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005123044/alto-campoo-fuente-del-chivo-climb" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Campoo - Fuente del Chivo Climb&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 3 hours and 20 minutes, while moderate loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004549582/montesclaros-sanctuary-and-alto-del-bardal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montesclaros Sanctuary and Alto del Bardal Loop&lt;/a&gt; can take just under 3 hours and 20 minutes.
tips: Beautiful 12th century church. and an example of Romanesque-style religious architecture in southern Cantabria. The church is listed as an Asset of Cultural Interest. Inside it has a small interactive museum.

It is located almost on the border with the Palencia mountain. It has an altitude of 1092 meters, a distance of 4 km, and the average slope is 5.65%. It is a wide road in perfect condition and with little traffic. A beautiful setting and absolute tranquility. The views are wonderful.

Although somewhat unknown, this 17th century Sanctuary is a jewel of Cantabria. A place with charm and great tranquility, completely secluded in the mountains and with spectacular views in any direction. They even have accommodation and the butterfly exhibition is worth seeing.

Calle Mayor, at the end of its route, widens, forming a square where there is a public fountain in charge of supplying water to the population. It fulfilled this function until 1910, when the deposit was built. It simply consists of a column with a square base from which four water pipes supply. It is believed that it may date from the beginning of the 18th century. Next to the fountain are the Hermitage of San Roque and the Teatro Principal.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Fontibre, Villacantid, Alto de Bardal, Monasterio de Montes Claros, Reinosa circular route?
faqs.answer: This road cycling route is approximately 69 kilometers long and typically takes around 3 hours and 17 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your cycling speed and stops along the way.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is classified as a moderate road ride. It involves an elevation gain and descent of about 800 meters, requiring good fitness from cyclists. While primarily paved, some segments may be unpaved and challenging.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: You can find public parking options in towns like Reinosa or Fontibre, which are convenient starting points for this circular route. Specific parking areas are usually available near the town centers.

faqs.question: What are the surface conditions like on this route, and is a racebike suitable?
faqs.answer: While primarily a road cycling route, it's important to note that certain sections might be unpaved. The climb to Alto del Bardal, for instance, is on the paved CA-284 road with minimal traffic. However, given the possibility of unpaved segments, a racebike is generally suitable, but cyclists should be prepared for varied terrain. If you prefer more comfort on potentially rougher sections, a gravel bike could also be a good option.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse scenery and notable landmarks. You'll pass by the Church of Santa María la Mayor, ascend Alto del Bardal for spectacular views, visit Fontibre, the natural source of the Ebro River, and see the Sanctuary of Our Lady of Montesclaros, which houses a unique butterfly museum. The Aurora Fountain is also a highlight.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region of Cantabria generally offers pleasant cycling conditions during spring, summer, and early autumn. These seasons provide more stable weather and longer daylight hours, making for a more enjoyable experience on the route.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route that passes through various towns and natural areas. While there are no specific prohibitions against dogs on the roads themselves, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially in populated areas or near wildlife. Be mindful of local regulations in villages and at specific points of interest.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route, especially since it's in the Ebro Reservoir Special Protection Area for Birds?
faqs.answer: No specific cycling permits or entrance fees are required to cycle this route. While the route passes through the Ebro Reservoir Special Protection Area for Birds, general road cycling does not typically incur fees. However, always respect local wildlife and environmental regulations within protected areas.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other cycling or hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the Camino Olvidado (Bimón-Matamorisca), the Camino natural de Ebro - Cantabria, and the Asturian Way, among others. This offers opportunities to explore connected trails if you wish to extend your ride.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, you'll find various cafes, pubs, and accommodation options in the towns along or near the route, such as Reinosa, Fontibre, and Villacantid. These towns offer convenient stops for refreshments or overnight stays.

faqs.question: Are there any specific sections known for particularly tranquil cycling?
faqs.answer: Yes, the climb to Alto del Bardal, which is on the paved CA-284 road, is known for its minimal traffic. This contributes to a very tranquil cycling experience, allowing you to fully enjoy the spectacular landscapes of the Endino mountain and its dense forest.
